scope: repository-controlled report production and certification preparation

requirements.text: Comply with normative references
No issuer master key in kernel
Kernel does not generate ARQC/TC/AAC
CAPKs public key integrity only
PED-owned VERIFY model
Correct PSE/PPSE selection
Parse valid GPO response formats 77 and 80
Extract AIP and AFL or fail with explicit missing mandatory tag
Validate SFI and record range before READ RECORD
Encode READ RECORD P2 as (SFI << 3) | 0x04
Parse record templates into card data without unmasked sensitive logging
Reject inconsistent or malformed PAN and Track 2 data without partial data-store updates
Symbolic constants for TVR
TVR cleared before each transaction
RFU bits not set
TSI bits set correctly
Terminal Capabilities 9F33 supplied through stable ABI and included in DOL data
Additional Terminal Capabilities 9F40 supplied through stable ABI and included in DOL data
Terminal Transaction Qualifiers 9F66 supplied through stable ABI and included in contactless DOL data
CID decode with mask 0xC0
CVM List parsing and limits
TVR byte3 bits on CVM outcome
Use certified CVM code table
Terminal and PED CVM capabilities supplied through stable ABI
CVM Results stored as three-byte EMV object
Distinguish offline plaintext offline enciphered and online PIN methods
No clear PIN values exposed to kernel memory
Delegate PIN block construction to PED or secure PIN module
P1 encoding: 0x00/0x40/0x80
CDA request not colliding with type bits
CDA request profile-defined
Fetch IACs from card with signed profile fallback for absent tags
TACs from config
TAA decision order
Deterministic fallback from profile
State-specific SW handling
No generic non-9000 error
CAPK hash verification
CAPK integrity not confidentiality
Issuer cert recovery failure TVR
ICC cert recovery failure TVR
SDA failure TVR bit
DDA failure TVR bit
CDA failure TVR and no fallback
CDA exact verification
INTERNAL AUTHENTICATE for DDA uses DDOL values
DDA signed dynamic data verified with recovered ICC public key
Reject placeholder malformed or incomplete ODA certification vectors
Process tag 91 issuer authentication data
Set TSI only when issuer authentication is attempted
Set TVR issuer authentication failed on card failure
Construct CDOL2 from card/profile data and host response data
Include issuer authentication or authorization response data where required by CDOL2
Issue second GENERATE AC where required after online authorization
Treat final TC as approval and final AAC as decline
Validate issuer script template structure
Execute issuer script commands in order
Capture issuer script command SW1/SW2
Set before-final-GAC script failure TVR bit
Set after-final-GAC script failure TVR bit
Report issuer script phase and SW1/SW2 results to Level 3 for host reporting
C-8 kernel for contactless
Outcome parameters callback
C-8 not contact kernel
Reject example-only profiles from certification or production loading
Keep scheme profile and certification variable data out of production kernel source
Non-re-entrant
Buffer ownership
Bounded callback timeouts
Stable error codes retrievable after terminal outcome
PED API returns status and secure handles only
Online PIN encrypted blocks are not copied into general kernel memory
Formal log policy
Obtain unpredictable numbers from approved platform RNG callback
Reject all-zero, all-FF, or repeated unpredictable numbers
Define every error code in a stable ABI table
Fail closed for unknown or unpermitted callback failures
Support signed configuration updates
Reject rollback or replayed configuration versions
Apply configuration updates atomically
Retain versioned configuration identity and profile byte hash in transaction logs
EMVCo L2 certification
Penetration test
SHALL pass automated schema validation before release.
SHALL not contain semantic contradictions such as next_state = SE with an action saying "jump to TAA."
SHALL classify DOLs as DOL values, not constructed TLV templates.
SHALL reject placeholder values, ellipses, non-hex material, dummy AIDs, dummy RIDs, and dummy CAPKs from any production or certification profile.
SHALL contain complete cryptographic vectors. Scenario summaries are not test vectors.
SHALL construct all APDUs according to ISO/IEC 7816-4 and EMV command-specific rules.
SHALL handle APDU response status words by command and state, not by a single global success/failure rule.
SHALL implement at least the following status-word categories where applicable:
SHALL validate abi_version and struct_size before dereferencing optional fields.
SHALL fail initialization if mandatory callbacks are absent.
SHALL define every amount in minor units and SHALL bind currency exponent handling to terminal/acquirer configuration.
SHALL define all bitmaps using symbolic constants and one canonical mapping file. Implementation code SHALL NOT use unexplained raw bitmap literals.
SHALL reject CAPKs whose signed configuration integrity cannot be verified.
SHALL treat expired CAPKs as unavailable unless a lab-approved test profile explicitly requires expiry simulation.
SHALL obtain EMV Level 2 approval for every claimed interface, kernel, and scheme profile.
requirements.evidence_artifact: conformance::tests::conformance_statement_json_is_deterministic_and_scoped; provenance::tests::provenance_manifest_rejects_resource_limits; provenance::tests::provenance_manifest_rejects_ambiguous_artifact_names; traceability_foundation::lab_manifest_and_provenance_cover_reproducible_build_artifacts; traceability_foundation::krn_ref_001_conformance_statement_declares_normative_hierarchy; external EMV Level 2 approval and signed LoA required

requirements.text: SHALL not present illustrative profiles or placeholder vectors as certification evidence.
SHALL verify digital signature before using any configuration data.
SHALL reject configurations with invalid schema, unknown mandatory fields, expired CAPKs, invalid AID encoding, invalid hex strings, non-hex key material, or invalid terminal parameter lengths.
SHALL implement rollback protection using monotonic versioning or secure counters.
SHALL preserve non-type CID bits for trace logging and scheme/profile handling, but SHALL NOT use them to change cryptogram type classification unless the licensed specification/profile requires it.
SHALL use PPSE 2PAY.SYS.DDF01 for contactless Entry Point.
SHALL return structured contactless outcome data rather than only generic approve/decline status.
SHALL evaluate contactless transaction limit, CVM limit, and floor limit using signed scheme/acquirer configuration.
SHALL treat CDCVM as profile-defined. The kernel SHALL NOT assume that any single tag or bit universally proves CDCVM without scheme/profile validation.
SHALL implement relay-resistance APDUs, timing constraints, and result handling only where the licensed profile requires or certifies them.
SHALL NOT treat CDCVM as a universal method code unless the active contactless profile explicitly defines that mapping.
SHALL construct PDOL, CDOL1, CDOL2, DDOL, and TDOL data by resolving each requested tag from the terminal/card/kernel context and appending the exact requested length.
SHALL use zero padding or profile-defined defaults only where permitted by EMV and scheme rules.
SHALL implement the full transition table as a machine-readable artifact with RFC 4180 valid CSV or JSON.
SHALL distinguish fatal protocol errors from risk conditions. A missing mandatory tag may be fatal in one state and TVR-mediated in another only if the licensed standard permits that behavior.
SHALL preserve enough context to replay a transaction deterministically for certification debugging, excluding clear PIN and excluded sensitive data.
SHALL process card removal, APDU timeout, host timeout, and callback failure through explicitly documented transitions.
SHALL construct CDOL1 and CDOL2 data exactly from the active DOL definition.
SHALL encode requested cryptogram type using 0x00, 0x40, or 0x80 request bits, plus only licensed/profile-defined lower-bit flags.
SHALL NOT use 0x80 as a "first GENERATE AC flag."
SHALL parse returned CID by CID & 0xC0 and shall parse AC, ATC, IAD, and any response template data according to EMV/profile rules.
SHALL build CDOL1 from tag 8C where present, or from profile-defined defaults where permitted.
SHALL include the unpredictable number, TVR, transaction amount, date, type, terminal country, CVM results, and other requested data exactly as CDOL1 requires.
SHALL request AAC, TC, or ARQC according to TAA.
SHALL parse response format 1 or 2 as permitted by the active profile.
SHALL if CDA was requested, verify CDA before accepting the returned cryptogram outcome.
SHALL separate contact and contactless kernel selection in configuration.
SHALL NOT treat Book C-8 as a contact kernel unless a specific licensed specification and certification approval explicitly support that use.
SHOULD use Book C-8 for contactless where the product, scheme set, region, terminal class, and certification scope support C-8.
SHALL reject a transaction if no certified kernel/profile mapping exists for the selected AID and explicit interface.
SHALL disable full APDU logging in production unless certified support mode is enabled by policy.
SHALL exclude cardholder data, clear PIN, PIN block, and secret material from crash dumps.
SHALL make debug builds cryptographically and operationally distinguishable from production builds.
SHALL pass online authorization data to Level 3 without performing acquirer/issuer host role.
SHALL receive host response data, including authorization response code, issuer authentication data, and issuer scripts, through the Level 3 callback/API.
SHALL measure ODA RSA/ECC operations separately from TLV parsing and APDU overhead.
SHALL define certification performance targets in the product profile, not in generic prose.
SHALL interpret 63 Cx from VERIFY as PIN verification warning with tries remaining and update TVR/CVM results accordingly.
SHALL reject placeholder strings such as ..., dummy RIDs, dummy AIDs, and non-hex CAPK values in production or certification configuration.
SHALL validate all CAPK modulus/checksum strings as hex and expected length before loading.
SHALL evaluate processing restrictions in the order required by EMV and scheme profile.

SHALL NOT create non-standard TVR bits for currency mismatch or service mismatch. The implementation shall express such conditions using the EMV-defined AUC/service/version/date conditions or profile-defined terminal decision inputs.
SHALL use 2PAY.SYS.DDF01 for contactless PPSE selection.
SHALL support direct AID selection when PSE/PPSE is absent, unsupported, or profile rules require direct selection.
SHALL evaluate denial before online approval requests.
SHALL include IAC values in TAA whenever available or required by the application/profile.
SHALL NOT allow an unconstrained configuration field such as default_cryptogram to override EMV or scheme rules.
SHALL parse primitive and constructed BER-TLV objects according to EMV and ISO/IEC 7816-4 rules.
SHALL parse DOLs as tag-length sequences, not as constructed TLV templates.
SHALL reject malformed tags, unsupported indefinite length encoding, lengths exceeding configured maxima, truncated values, or invalid nested templates.

SHALL maintain a machine-readable TLV catalogue with columns: tag, name, type, length rule, source, interface applicability, scheme applicability, presence rule, sensitive-data classification, and test IDs.
SHALL mark scheme-specific, proprietary, and RFU tags as PROFILE-DEFINED and shall not assign invented semantics.

SHALL admit card-originated AFL record TLVs only from direct primitive Template 70 children and shall reject terminal-owned, kernel-owned, host-response-owned, GENERATE AC, or dynamic-authentication tags without partially updating the transaction data store.
SHALL implement floor-limit checking per profile and transaction type.
SHALL implement random transaction selection only according to EMV/profile rules. The algorithm and parameters shall be certified as part of the profile.
SHALL maintain offline counters only in non-volatile storage where the active scheme/profile requires terminal-maintained counters.
SHALL set TSI terminal risk management performed when TRM has executed.
SHALL set TSI bits only after the corresponding function has actually been performed.

artifacts.boundary: lab test-case crosswalk remains external

required_reports.title: 100% unit coverage report
required_reports.required_evidence: submitted commit, tool versions, target, feature set, coverage metadata JSON, and HTML/XML or lab-accepted report

required_reports.title: Full EMV integration report
required_reports.required_evidence: test-tool version, profile set, device firmware, APDU traces, outcomes, deviations, and disposition

required_reports.title: Static-analysis report
required_reports.required_evidence: accepted tool version, command lines, findings, remediations, and residual-risk acceptance

required_reports.title: Fuzzing/no-crash report
required_reports.required_evidence: engine versions, corpus hashes, run budgets, coverage/path metrics, crashes, and dispositions

required_reports.title: Signed conformance template and approval artifact
required_reports.required_evidence: recognized lab or authority-signed template tied to submitted binary, profile, and device scope

required_reports.title: Device, L1, and PCI/PED evidence
required_reports.required_evidence: target device approval, reader/L1 evidence, and PCI PTS/PED integration statement

open_gates.authority: EMVCo, scheme, acquirer, and recognized laboratory
open_gates.required_attachment: final lab execution report plus signed approval or LoA-equivalent artifact for the claimed interface, kernel, and scheme scope
open_gates.required_metadata: submitted binary hash, profile hash, test-tool package, device model, firmware, and claimed interface list
open_gates.acceptance_gate: approval artifact agrees with the submitted binary, profile set, device scope, and RTM

open_gates.authority: scheme, acquirer, or laboratory profile authority
open_gates.required_attachment: signed AID, TAC/IAC, limit, CVM, CDA-control, and kernel-selection profile bundle
open_gates.required_metadata: profile version, issuer authority, retrieval date, signature status, rollback policy, and SHA-256
open_gates.acceptance_gate: signed profile bundle is loaded through the verified profile path and matches trace identity

open_gates.required_attachment: approved CAPK set with RID, key index, modulus, exponent, expiry, checksum, and provenance
open_gates.required_metadata: CAPK source, retrieval date, expiry date, checksum, bundle hash, and approval reference
open_gates.acceptance_gate: CAPK bundle passes repository checksum gates and traces to accepted public-key material

open_gates.authority: recognized laboratory or scheme test-vector authority
open_gates.required_attachment: complete lab-supplied SDA, DDA, and CDA cryptographic vectors with expected TVR/TSI and outcome data
open_gates.required_metadata: vector class, vector source, tool version, expected outputs, method coverage, and bundle hash
open_gates.acceptance_gate: docs/oda_test_vectors.json is replaced by vector_class CERTIFICATION and passes complete-vector validation
open_gates.repository_support: src/oda.rs, docs/oda_test_vectors.json, ODA parser and validation tests

open_gates.authority: EMVCo contactless kernel/product approval path and scheme profile authority
open_gates.required_attachment: lab-selected C-8 version and bulletin reconciliation, profile data, contactless test-tool results, and outcome traces
open_gates.required_metadata: C-8 version, bulletin set, included/excluded adjacent books, test-tool version, terminal profile, TTQ source, and trace pack hash
open_gates.acceptance_gate: contactless claim is tied to the accepted C-8 package, profile set, device/L1 evidence, and masked outcome traces

open_gates.authority: device vendor, L1 laboratory, scheme, and EMVCo process as applicable
open_gates.required_attachment: target terminal, contact interface, contactless reader, firmware, and L1/device certification evidence
open_gates.required_metadata: model, hardware revision, firmware, reader stack, L1 approval reference, and submitted binary/profile hash
open_gates.acceptance_gate: device evidence names the same target, firmware, binary, profile bundle, and interface scope as the submission
open_gates.repository_support: docs/lab_submission_manifest.md, trace identity metadata, C ABI integration examples

open_gates.authority: PCI SSC, PCI-recognized laboratory, PED vendor, and security assessor
open_gates.required_attachment: PCI PTS POI integration statement, PED-owned VERIFY evidence, and no-clear-PIN custody review
open_gates.required_metadata: PTS listing or assessment reference, PED model, firmware, PIN method, opaque-handle policy, and integration evidence
open_gates.acceptance_gate: PED evidence confirms the kernel only handles opaque PIN handles and cannot log or receive clear PIN data

open_gates.authority: independent security assessor accepted for the product submission
open_gates.required_attachment: penetration test and architecture review report covering APDU injection, state-machine bypass, trace leakage, and profile tampering
open_gates.required_metadata: scope, commit hash, device/profile set, findings, remediations, residual-risk owner, and retest evidence
open_gates.acceptance_gate: all critical/high findings are remediated or formally accepted before final certification-facing release

open_gates.authority: submission owner, laboratory, scheme, or acquirer acceptance path
open_gates.required_attachment: 100% unit coverage report plus coverage metadata JSON and full EMV test-plan integration report for the submitted build
open_gates.required_metadata: source commit, Cargo version, Rust compiler version, target triple, feature set, cargo-llvm-cov version, threshold, enforcement mode, profile hash, CAPK hash, test-tool version, report hash, and deviations
open_gates.acceptance_gate: coverage and integration reports match the submitted binary, profiles, CAPKs, vectors, and annex hashes

open_gates.authority: submission owner, laboratory, scheme, or acquirer acceptance path
open_gates.required_attachment: accepted static-analysis report and fuzzing/no-crash report with findings and dispositions
open_gates.required_metadata: tool versions, commands, sanitizer set, corpus hashes, run budget, crashes, remediations, and accepted residual risks
open_gates.acceptance_gate: reports show no unresolved unacceptable findings for the submitted source, binary, and parser surfaces

open_gates.authority: EMVCo, recognized laboratory, scheme, or acquirer as applicable
open_gates.required_attachment: signed conformance statement template or approval package tied to the claimed scope
open_gates.required_metadata: template version, signer, signature date, submitted binary hash, ABI version, RTM version, and device/profile scope
open_gates.acceptance_gate: signed template agrees with the ABI JSON statement, RTM, lab manifest, and approval artifact
